steps.text: Parse the setup. The rectangle has width $w$ feet and a length that is 49 times the width, so length $= 49w$ feet. Area $=$ length $\times$ width $= (49w)(w) = y$.
Identify the role of $49w$ in the expression $y=(49w)(w)$. One factor is $w$ (width) and the other factor $49w$ must be the other dimension, which is the length.

Evaluate each choice:
  • Choice A: The length of the rectangle, in feet — since length $= 49\times w = 49w$, this is exactly what $49w$ represents. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • Choice B: The area of the rectangle, in square feet — the area is $y$, not $49w$. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: The difference between length and width — that would be $49w - w = 48w$, not $49w$. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: The width of the rectangle, in feet — $w$ is the width; $49w$ is 49 times larger. Incorrect.
